rationale: The cited paper is a measurement-method study (single-item vs. multiple-item predictive validity in ad copy tests), which is topically different from the citing paper’s attachment/closeness/loyalty focus, so it may be only a methodological support citation. However, the matched evidence is only a DOI-level reference with no in-text context, and such a paper could plausibly be used to justify scale design choices, so it cannot be safely dropped as obvious mention-only.
